response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of solid individual-contributor experience with genuine relevance to engineering productivity — CI/CD infrastructure setup, custom testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), MCP/AI-agent tooling, and a consistent habit of knowledge-sharing through workshops and conference talks. However, this is an explicit people-management role requiring at least 2 years of managing engineers, and the resume contains zero evidence of direct reports, performance conversations, headcount decisions, or org-level initiative leadership. Every position listed is an IC role. The technical foundation is real, but the management gap is disqualifying for a Level II Manager opening.
strengths: Hands-on developer tooling background spanning CI/CD pipeline setup, custom D-language testing frameworks, and MCP/AI-agent infrastructure — directly aligned with the team's engineering productivity mandate

Demonstrated knowledge-sharing culture: organized recurring Refactoring Dojos and Code Katas, co-created a multi-module Selenium course for a regional IT cluster, and delivered conference talks — signals coaching aptitude

Broad multi-domain technical depth (web, iOS/Apple TV, embedded, low-latency microservices) provides the cross-platform credibility needed to evaluate designs and guide engineers across a mobile-platform ecosystem
concerns: No people management experience whatsoever — no mention of direct reports, hiring decisions, performance reviews, or team leadership in any of the 14 positions listed
No evidence of strategic or organizational leadership: roadmap ownership, cross-team alignment, and leading organizational change are entirely absent from the career history
All titles are individual-contributor (Software Developer, Senior Software Developer, Staff Software Engineer) with no progression toward or past experience in engineering management

key_factors.factor: Relevant developer tooling and productivity experience
